data.title: Dow Jones Climbs as CPI Inflation Cools to 9-Month Low
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) is set to open Friday higher following an inflation update from the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS).

data.title: U.S. Stock Hover Around Flatline After January CPI Report
data.description:
U.S. stock futures hovered around the flatline on Friday morning as traders digested the January 2026 Consumer Price Index (CPI) data. The Bureau of Labor Statistics reported that the consumer price index rose 0.2% in January, reflecting a 2.4% gain on an annualized basis. Economists polled by Dow Jones expected a 0.3% month-over-month rise and a 2.5% year-over-year gain. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up by 0.14%, 0.09%, and 0.07%, respectively, at 8:55 a.m. EST on February 13.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During Thursday's regular trading, all three indexes closed lower amid growing fears that artificial intelligence (AI) is slowly taking over other sectors such as real estate, trucking, and software. The S&P 500 fell 1.6%, the Nasdaq Composite dropped 2%, and the DJIA slipped 1.3%.

Turning to trending stocks, shares of electric vehicle (EV) maker Rivian Automotive (RIVN) rallied 21% in Friday's pre-market trading after the company reported better-than-expected Q4 results and issued impressive guidance. Rivian expects 2026 vehicle deliveries in the range of 62,000 to 67,000 units, reflecting a 47% to 59% increase.

Also, Applied Materials (AMAT) stock jumped 11% after reporting strong earnings and guidance. Coinbase (COIN) stock surged 7% as the crypto company reported a 156% surge in its 2025 trading volume to $5.2 trillion. In contrast, Pinterest (PINS) stock tanked 22% following the social media platform's weak quarterly results and a soft forecast.

data.title: Why Is Mint Stock (MIMI) Up Today?
data.description:
Mint (MIMI) stock rocketed higher on Friday despite a lack of news from the interior design and fitworks company. There are no new press releases or filings with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) that explain why the stock is up today.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Instead, traders will want to turn to the statistics of MIMI stock to understand what's behind the rally. That includes the stock's unusual trading activity, with more than 37 million shares traded as of this writing. For comparison, Mint stock's three-month daily average trading volume is about 1.63 million shares. This increased trading activity helps to explain why MIMI stock has rallied today.

To go along with this, investors will note that Mint is a low-float stock at some 12.7 million shares. MIMI is also a penny stock, as it last closed with a price of 28 cents and traded for about 46 cents at the time of this writing. These factors, combined with the heavy trading volume, also shed light on why the stock has rallied today, as low-float penny stocks are often subject to extreme volatility when retail traders take an interest in them.

Mint Stock Movement Today

Mint stock was up 64.68% in pre-market trading on Friday, following a 10.66% fall yesterday. The shares have also dropped 6.67% year-to-date and 94.12% over the past 12 months.
data.author: William White

data.title: Iren Stock Reverses Plunge — Here's What Analysts See Next
data.description:
Data center operator Iren Limited's (IREN) shares rose about 3% during Friday's pre-market trading after plummeting over 6% the previous day. The gain comes as the Sydney-based company announced its upcoming inclusion in the MSCI USA Index.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

What Drove Iren's Sharp Sell-Off?

The MSCI USA Index is a major U.S. stock market benchmark that tracks about 600 large- and mid-cap publicly traded American companies. The index is closely watched by international and institutional investors.

The plunge in Iren's shares on Thursday mirrored the drop seen in Big Tech on the same day, as investors fretted about persistently monstrous spending on artificial intelligence by major American firms such as Alphabet (GOOGL), Meta (META), and Microsoft (MSFT). This is not particularly surprising, as analysts have frequently emphasized that Iren's shares are traded as a proxy for the AI infrastructure buildout.

Is a New Rally Incoming?

Lately, Iren's shares have significantly underperformed, tumbling about 17% on February 4.

The sell-off deepened the following day, with the stock dropping a further 11%-plus after the company posted Q2 2026 results that revealed a larger-than-expected loss. Adding to this, revenue fell 23% sequentially but climbed 59% year-over-year to $184.7 billion.

However, as of 8:33 a.m. EST on Friday, Iren's shares ticked up about 3%, which might mark the start of a new rally. This comes as analysts lately have addressed the cause of the post-earnings rout. Bernstein analyst Gautam Chhugani argued that it is a matter of when, not if, Iren will be able to add new deals on top of its $9.7 billion contract with Microsoft (MSFT).

In addition, Canaccord Genuity's Joseph Vafi has pointed to Iren's new 1.6 gigawatt site in Oklahoma, while Cantor Fitzgerald analyst Brett Knoblauch sees no reason to be worried about Iren's preparation to take its 1.4 gigawatt site in Nolan County, Texas, live in April.

Is IREN Stock a Strong Buy?

Across Wall Street, IREN's shares remain a Moderate Buy based on analysts' consensus rating. This breaks down to nine Buys, two Holds, and one Sell issued by 12 analysts over the past three months.

However, at $79.80, the average IREN price target indicates almost 100% upside from current trading levels.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Nebius (NBIS) Stock Down after Q4 Earnings — Here's What Crowd Wisdom Shows
data.description:
Nebius Group (NBIS) stock trended lower in early Friday trading after the AI cloud infrastructure provider reported fourth-quarter results that missed revenue estimates, even though sales were higher than a year earlier. TipRanks' Crowd Wisdom data also points to continued investor caution toward the stock, adding to the pressure on the stock.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Key Takeaways from Nebius' Q4

Nebius reported revenue of about $227.7 million for the quarter, sharply higher than a year ago but below expectations. Losses of $173 million also widened from the year-ago quarter as the company increased spending to expand its data center capacity.

Management said demand for its AI computing services remains strong. The company ended the year with about 170 megawatts of active power and plans to reach 800 megawatts to 1 gigawatt of connected capacity by the end of 2026. Nebius also reported annual recurring revenue of around $1.25 billion and posted a positive adjusted EBITDA quarter, showing higher usage of its platform.

However, investors appeared focused on costs and execution. Operating expenses climbed significantly as the company invested in new data center infrastructure, and the business is still far from profitability.

What Does TipRanks' Crowd Wisdom Show?

TipRanks' Crowd Wisdom data shows caution among retail investors. Based on activity from more than 852,000 tracked portfolios, investors' sentiment toward Nebius is currently Negative.

About 2.3% of tracked portfolios hold NBIS shares, with investors allocating an average of 4.69% of their portfolios to the stock. However, recent activity shows investors have been reducing exposure. The number of portfolios holding NBIS rose slightly over the past seven days but declined about 1.8% over the last 30 days.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

Datadog (DDOG) – Datadog is a cloud software company that monitors apps and IT systems. Today, Jefferies analyst Brent Thill maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a price target of $160. Interestingly, 20 out of the 21 Top Analysts who recently rated the stock gave it a Buy. Taken together, their 12-month price targets imply an upside of about 45.69%.

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 2/13/26 – U.S. Stock Futures Wobble Ahead of Key CPI Data
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were mixed early Friday as traders anticipate the key consumer price index (CPI) data this morning. Economists forecast inflation rising 2.5% year-over-year and 0.3% from the previous month. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) and the S&P 500 (SPX) were up 0.13% and 0.02%, respectively, while those on the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.13% at 6:10 a.m. EST on February 13.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During Thursday's regular trading, all three indexes closed lower amid growing fears that AI is slowly taking over other sectors such as real estate, trucking, and software, after hitting software and finance stocks earlier in the week. The S&P 500 fell 1.6%, the Nasdaq Composite dropped 2%, and the DJIA slipped 1.3%.

After markets closed, Applied Materials (AMAT) jumped 11% after reporting strong earnings and guidance. Airbnb (ABNB) gained around 2% on upbeat outlook comments, while Pinterest (PINS) slid 14% following weak quarterly results and a soft forecast. Moreover, Apple (AAPL) dropped 5%, recording its biggest one-day decline since April 2025, on news that it may delay its planned AI Siri upgrade.

On the earnings front, notable companies including Moderna (MRNA) and Wendy's (WEN) will report earnings today.

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was down, floating near 4.12%. WTI crude oil futures were trending higher, hovering near $62.96 per barrel as of the last check. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price decreased to nearly $4,973 per ounce on Friday.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened mixed today amid a flurry of mixed corporate earnings.

Asia-Pacific Markets Traded Lower

Asia-Pacific markets traded lower on February 13, tracking Wall Street losses.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index fell 1.72%. In China, the Shanghai Composite dropped 1.26%, while the Shenzhen Component lost 1.41%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei fell 1.21%, and the Topix decreased 1.63%.
